channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Modi, BJP influence grow in leaps and bounds across India
item.description: How Prime Minister Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation.
item.dc:creator: Tavleen Singh and Bhat Burhan

media:group.media:description: Narendra Modi says the latest election shows India's democracy is vibrant.

item.title: Police make arrest over alleged stabbing of man sleeping at home in NT town
item.description: A 23-year-old man has been arrested over the alleged stabbing of another man who was sleeping in a home in remote Tennant Creek about 990 kilometres south-east of Darwin.

media:group.media:description: NT police have arrested a 23-year-old man in Tennant Creek over the alleged stabbing of another man in town.

item.title: Scottish ref under police protection as Celtic penalty sparks threats
item.description: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

media:group.media:description: Referee John Beaton caused a storm of controversy by awarding a penalty in added time between Celtic and Motherwell in the Scottish Premier League.

item.title: Kalgoorlie's 'shovel ready' water project misses out in federal budget
item.description: A project designed to drought-proof Australia's biggest outback city has missed out on funding in this week's federal budget, with the City of Kalgoorlie-Boulder set to review its ambitious plans for what has been dubbed Water Bank.
item.dc:creator: Jarrod Lucas and Ivo da Silva

media:group.media:description: Kalgoorlie-Boulder is Australia's biggest outback city with a population of about 30,000 people.

item.title: Live: Crows chasing third straight win against out-of-form Roos
item.description: The eighth-placed Crows are in a confident mood as they host the Kangaroos, who are desperately looking to avoid a fourth consecutive defeat. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton and Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: The pressure is on early at Adelaide Oval as the Crows try to lift after a tough start.

item.title: Breaking: Man critical after shark attack off Rottnest Island
item.description: A man in his thirties has been seriously injured by a shark off Rottnest Island, on Perth's coast.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Williams

media:group.media:description: The man was attacked near Geordie Bay on the north side of Rottnest Island.

item.title: Where to watch Delta Goodrem's Eurovision final performance
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.
item.dc:creator: Dannielle Maguire

media:group.media:description: Delta Goodrem will be flying the flag for Australia this weekend.

item.title: 'The sword is still hanging': Farmers in limbo as Inland Rail land sits idle
item.description: For more than a decade, Australians in the path of the proposed Inland Rail corridor have wondered if the nation-building project will force them from their homes. Although the northern section has been scrapped, the legacy of its blueprints is still fuelling anxiety.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Long

media:group.media:description: Tim Durre grows crops for hay at his farm in Charlton, Queensland.

item.title: Fury at bill increasing environmental water flows over private property
item.description: Politicians and landholders vow to fight a controversial new New South Wales law that will allow the inundation of private property from environmental water flows under the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter, Cara Jeffery, and Nicola Ceccato

media:group.media:description: Deb Blackwell is a grazier at Mundarlo, on the Murrumbidgee River upstream of Wagga Wagga.

item.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes
item.description: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Breen

media:group.media:description: Vigils held for Kumanjayi Little Baby have been followed by fierce debate over child protection laws.

item.title: PM says fuel supply now above pre-war levels, no word on extending excise cut
item.description: Australia's fuel supply is now greater than it was before the Iran war broke out in late February, the prime minister has said, while refusing to indicate whether the fuel excise cut will be extended.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Bowles

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese says the country's fuel supply is now greater than it was before the Iran war.

item.title: Frustrated Scheffler slams 'absurd' pin positions after disappointing round
item.description: Scottie Scheffler takes issue with the pin positions at the PGA Championship after being upstaged by an American duo.

media:group.media:description: Scottie Scheffler cut a frustrated figure after finishing the second round of the PGA Championshuip tied for ninth.

item.title: The southern Sydney bay residents say is becoming a mudflat
item.description: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: Travis Read says the bay has about 15 years before it becomes a mudflat.

item.title: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians
item.description: Seven Palestinians, including a child, have died in Friday's air strikes, which Israel said targeted Hamas militant leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad.

media:group.media:description: Israel has not confirmed whether Hamas militant leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad has been killed or wounded.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians unlikely to be paid out, experts say
item.description: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Perpitch

media:group.media:description: The passengers and the minibus driver were travelling to the quarantine facility on Friday.

item.title: First Nations man's bedtime story for his sons revives dying language
item.description: Five years ago, Gudjala man Braithen Knox began learning his mob's language, which has fewer than 10 speakers. Now a father of two, he is passing it on to his sons through bedtime stories.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Tindall

media:group.media:description: Braithen Knox wrote Where is the Dark Emu? for his sons to teach them Gudjal language from a young age.

item.title: A house fire cost Patrick and Susan everything, including their two dogs
item.description: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.
item.dc:creator: Becc Bird and Josh Brine

media:group.media:description: The couple's dogs, Mercy and Oscar, died as a result of the fire.

item.title: Advocates say 'three big gorillas' have stopped real national gambling reform
item.description: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

media:group.media:description: The NRL is seen as one of three 'big gorillas' blocking gambling reform.

item.title: Video of shark decapitation sparks calls for better protection
item.description: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.
item.dc:creator: Will Corbett

media:group.media:description: An expert has called for limits to the size of sharks that can be legally caught in WA.

item.title: From colonisation to 'safe space': The story of Dhirri-aay-aay
item.description: Back on country, Aunty Polly Cutmore says she feels like a child again when visiting her ancestral lands. Now, as a respected Goombri elder, she shares the stories of her country.
item.dc:creator: Brigitte Murphy

media:group.media:description: Aunty Polly Cutmore shares the emotional connection and history behind her traditional lands.

item.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day
item.description: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.
item.dc:creator: Elise Kinsella

media:group.media:description: Ms Ashton has been campaigning for stronger gambling regulations since her husband's death.

item.title: Dance helped stroke survivor Laura find confidence in her new body
item.description: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.
item.dc:creator: Selina Bryan

media:group.media:description: Laura Della Pasqua (L), MADE (Mature artists Dance Experience) Dance Participant at the Moonah Arts Centre in Hobart on 14 May, 2026.

item.title: Whether they're faded, frayed or stained, every hat tells a story
item.description: For many, hats are more than a way to keep the sun off your face; they tell stories and hold memories from far and wide. Five men share their collections.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson

media:group.media:description: Joe Caruana stacks his hats together in his shed.

item.title: 'Grossly incompetent': Action taken against Latham after online outburst
item.description: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: Mark Latham attacked the NSW Civil and Administrative Tribunal in a series of social media posts.

item.title: First memorial to Australia's deadliest Vietnam War battle unveiled
item.description: The longest and deadliest battle for Australian troops in the Vietnam War has finally been memorialised with a statue to the Battle of Coral–Balmoral erected in Gundagai.
item.dc:creator: Jess Scully

media:group.media:description: A statue depicting Richard Norden VC drag-carrying his section commander Joe Kelly back from enemy lines.
